lesson.title: Peppa Pig: Muddy Puddles
lesson.summary: In this excerpt, Peppa and George learn to wear boots before jumping in muddy puddles after the rain stops. The family cleans up and plays together, emphasizing fun and safety.

grammar_concepts.name: Imperatives
grammar_concepts.structure_rules: Base form of the verb (no subject, often with 'let's' for suggestions)
grammar_concepts.context_usage: To give commands, instructions, or suggestions; commonly used in friendly or parent-child interactions.
grammar_concepts.learning_tips: Imperatives can sound direct; adding 'please' softens them. Note that 'let's' is a contraction of 'let us' and suggests joint action.
grammar_concepts.explanation: Base form of the verb (no subject, often with 'let's' for suggestions)

grammar_concepts.name: Present Continuous
grammar_concepts.context_usage: To describe actions happening right now or around the current time; also used for temporary situations and future arrangements.
grammar_concepts.learning_tips: Common mistake: forgetting the auxiliary 'be' or using the base form of the verb. Pronunciation: '-ing' is often reduced to '-in'' in casual speech (e.g., 'jumpin'').
examples_from_transcript: Peppa and George are having a lot of fun.

grammar_concepts.name: Modal 'must' for necessity
grammar_concepts.structure_rules: Subject + must + base form of verb (no 'to')
grammar_concepts.context_usage: To express a strong obligation or necessity, often from an external rule or authority.
grammar_concepts.learning_tips: 'Must' is stronger than 'have to'; it does not change form for different subjects. Negative: 'must not' (mustn't) meaning prohibition.
grammar_concepts.explanation: Subject + must + base form of verb (no 'to')
examples_from_transcript: I must check if it's safe for you.

vocabulary.definition: A small pool of liquid, especially rainwater, on the ground.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Peppa loves jumping in muddy puddles.

vocabulary.definition: Strong shoes that cover the foot and ankle, often worn for outdoor activities.
Covered with or consisting of mud.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Peppa loves jumping in muddy puddles.

vocabulary.definition: A dirty or untidy state or condition.
vocabulary.example_sentence: And look at the mess you're in.

vocabulary.definition: Not likely to cause harm; free from danger or risk.

exercises.question: What does 'puddle' mean?
exercises.explanation: A puddle is a small pool of liquid, often rainwater.

exercises.question: Peppa says: 'If you jump in muddy puddles, you must wear your ___ .'
exercises.explanation: Boots are strong shoes for outdoor activities, like jumping in puddles.

exercises.question: The word 'muddy' means clean and dry.
exercises.explanation: Muddy means covered with mud, which is wet and dirty, not clean and dry.

exercises.question: What does 'mess' mean in the story?
exercises.explanation: Mess refers to a dirty or untidy condition, like the mud on their clothes.

exercises.question: Daddy says: '___ along, you two.' (What word completes the imperative?)
exercises.explanation: Imperatives use the base form of the verb. 'Run along' is a command telling them to go.

exercises.question: Which sentence uses the present continuous correctly?
exercises.explanation: Present continuous is formed with subject + am/is/are + verb-ing. 'Peppa is jumping' describes an ongoing action.

exercises.question: In the story, Mummy says 'You must wear your boots.' This means it is optional.
exercises.explanation: 'Must' expresses strong necessity or obligation, so it is not optional; it is required.

exercises.question: At the beginning of the story, it is raining.
exercises.explanation: The narrator says 'It is raining today', so Peppa and George cannot play outside initially.

exercises.question: Who checks if the big puddle is safe for George?
exercises.explanation: Peppa says 'I must check if it's safe for you' before allowing George to jump into the big puddle.

exercises.question: Daddy Pig says: 'Oh, well. ___ only mud.'
exercises.explanation: Daddy uses the contraction 'It's' meaning 'It is' to minimize the mess.

source_text.full_text: I'm Peppa Pig. This is my little brother George. This is Mummy Pig. And this is Daddy Pig. Peppa Pig. Muddypuddles! I'm going to play with you. I'm going to play with you. It is raining today, so Peppa and George cannot play outside. Daddy, it stopped raining. Can we go out to play? Ha-ha! All right. Run along, you two. Pfft! HE CHUCKLES Peppa loves jumping in muddy puddles. I love muddy puddles. Peppa, if you jump in muddy puddles, you must wear your boots. Sorry, Mummy. George likes to jump in muddy puddles too. George, if you jump in muddy puddles, you must wear your boots. Peppa likes to look after her little brother, George. George, let's find some more puddles. Peppa and George are having a lot of fun. Peppa has found a little puddle. George has found a big puddle. Look, George. There's a really big puddle. George wants to jump into the big puddle first. Stop, George. I must check if it's safe for you. Good. It is safe for you. Sorry, George. It's only mud. Peppa and George love jumping in muddy puddles. Come on, George. Let's go show Daddy. Goodness me. Daddy, Daddy, guess what we've been doing? Let me think. Have you been watching television? No, no, Daddy. Have you just had a bath? No, no. I know. You've been jumping in muddy puddles. Yes, yes, Daddy. We've been jumping in muddy puddles. Ho, ho. And look at the mess you're in. Ooh. Oh, well. It's only mud. Let's clean up quickly before Mummy sees the mess. Daddy, when we've cleaned up, will you and Mummy come and play too? Yes, we can all play in the garden. Peppa and George are wearing their boots. Mummy and Daddy are wearing their boots. Peppa loves jumping up and down in muddy puddles. Everyone loves jumping up and down in muddy puddles. Oh, Daddy Pig, look at the mess you're in. It's only mud. Peppa Pig. Peppa Pig. Peppa Pig.
